assertionsTested.title: MCP server starts and responds to initialize
assertionsTested.reason: Server responds to initialize with protocolVersion, capabilities.tools, and serverInfo with name 'spikes-mcp' and version '0.3.0'

assertionsTested.title: Tools list returns all three tools
assertionsTested.reason: tools/list returns exactly 3 tools with valid inputSchema: get_spikes, get_element_feedback, get_hotspots

assertionsTested.title: get_spikes returns all spikes with no filters
assertionsTested.reason: Calling get_spikes with empty arguments returns formatted text listing all 7 spikes with id, type, page, rating, comments, reviewer, timestamp

evidence.value: stdio pipe with initialize + notifications/initialized + tools/call get_spikes {}
Found 7 spike(s): [a4385-mc] page on index.html (Rating: love...), [a4385-mc] element on index.html (Rating: no, Selector: .hero-title...), etc.

assertionsTested.title: get_spikes filters by page, rating, and unresolved
assertionsTested.reason: All filter combinations work correctly: page filter returns 4 spikes on index.html; rating='no' returns 2 spikes; unresolved_only returns 6 unresolved; combined filters AND correctly; non-matching returns 'No spikes found'

evidence.value: tools/call get_spikes with {page:'index.html'}, {rating:'no'}, {unresolved_only:true}, combined filters, and non-matching page
Found 1 spike(s) matching page='index.html' AND rating='no' AND unresolved_only=true
No spikes found matching the criteria. Clean slate, or wrong filters?

assertionsTested.title: get_element_feedback returns feedback for CSS selector
assertionsTested.reason: Selector match returns element-type spikes for that selector; non-matching returns 'No feedback found'; missing selector returns JSON-RPC error -32602 (invalid params)

evidence.value: Found 3 feedback item(s) for '.hero-title': [a4385-mc] element on index.html...
No feedback found for selector '.nonexistent'. Ghost town.

assertionsTested.title: get_hotspots returns elements sorted by feedback count
assertionsTested.reason: Returns hotspots sorted by descending count; limit parameter works (default 10, limit=1 returns only top); shows correct feedback item counts

evidence.value: Top 3 hotspot(s): 1. .hero-title (3 feedback items), 2. .submit-btn (1 feedback item), 3. .nav-button (1 feedback item)

frictions.description: MCP stdio transport requires staggered delays between JSON-RPC messages when piping via shell - pipe closing too early causes server to stop before processing subsequent requests
frictions.resolution: Added 0.3s delays between messages and 2s sleep at end to keep pipe open until all responses received

frictions.description: Fixture data initially used snake_case field names but Spike struct expects camelCase due to #[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
frictions.resolution: Updated fixture to use camelCase: projectKey, elementText, resolvedAt

frictions.description: Fixture data used 'spike_type' field name but Spike struct expects 'type' with #[serde(rename = "type")]
frictions.resolution: Updated fixture to use 'type' field with lowercase values 'page' and 'element'

summary: All 6 MCP core assertions passed. Server correctly implements JSON-RPC protocol with proper initialize handshake, returns 3 tools with valid schemas, and all tool operations (get_spikes with filters, get_element_feedback, get_hotspots) function as specified. Error handling returns appropriate JSON-RPC error codes for invalid params.
